nombre: Baya Voce | Relationship Repair Expert | Columbia University
bio:
If you want a healthy relationship, repair is non-negotiable. I help you learn how.
Supervision w/ Esther Perel
Couples Research @columbia @maps__org
descripcion:
Comment EFFORT for a free pdf teaching you what to do in this scenario. 
.
One of you is pushing for change, the other is holding space for what already feels okay. This isn’t incompatibility, it’s a common rhythm many couples get stuck in.

One partner feels alone in the effort to grow, the other feels pressured just for being themselves. It’s not about who’s right or wrong, but about seeing how different ways of loving show up.

If you’re the one chasing progress, try noticing the value in what’s already here without mistaking it for being stuck.

If you’re the one content with the now, take a small step toward curiosity and ask questions before the tension builds.

This dynamic isn’t a problem, it’s a chance to learn how to love across difference instead of push against it.

#relationships #growth #connection

nombre: Baya Voce | Relationship Repair Expert | Columbia University
bio:
If you want a healthy relationship, repair is non-negotiable. I help you learn how.
Supervision w/ Esther Perel
Couples Research @columbia @maps__org
descripcion:
Sometimes the hardest part is realizing you were asked to move on without anything actually being repaired.

#healing #selfawareness #relationships

nombre: Baya Voce | Relationship Repair Expert | Columbia University
bio:
If you want a healthy relationship, repair is non-negotiable. I help you learn how.
Supervision w/ Esther Perel
Couples Research @columbia @maps__org
descripcion:
Waiting for someone to change before you let yourself heal is its own kind of trap. The resentment lives in every moment since where you stayed silent, managed their reaction, and left your own needs sitting on the floor.

Your voice might shake and it might be really hard, but the alternative is hard too, and we can do hard things (thanks for that one, @glennondoyle).

#boundaries #selfawareness #relationships

nombre: Baya Voce | Relationship Repair Expert | Columbia University
bio:
If you want a healthy relationship, repair is non-negotiable. I help you learn how.
Supervision w/ Esther Perel
Couples Research @columbia @maps__org
descripcion:
Everyone’s looking for red flags in the other person. The lies, the hot and cold, the way they make you question yourself. But years can go by while you’re watching them and completely missing the one signal that actually matters, what your own body has been trying to tell you the whole time.

#relationships #redflag #selfawareness

nombre: Baya Voce | Relationship Repair Expert | Columbia University
bio:
If you want a healthy relationship, repair is non-negotiable. I help you learn how.
Supervision w/ Esther Perel
Couples Research @columbia @maps__org
descripcion:
You’re not bad at relationships. You just keep accepting what you secretly believe you deserve.
#growth #relationships #selflove

nombre: Baya Voce | Relationship Repair Expert | Columbia University
bio:
If you want a healthy relationship, repair is non-negotiable. I help you learn how.
Supervision w/ Esther Perel
Couples Research @columbia @maps__org
descripcion:
Here’s what nobody tells you about “relationship goals”: the magic isn’t in never breaking. It’s in how beautifully you rebuild. ✨

You can tell everything about a couple not by watching them at brunch, but by watching what happens when someone messes up. Do they retreat into separate corners or find their way back to each other’s humanity? ❤️

The couples who make it aren’t the ones who never disconnect. They’re the ones who’ve learned that disconnection is just the comma, not the period. Real intimacy lives in the aftermath when someone says “I was wrong” and actually means it. When you can be pissed off without making it dangerous. 

It’s a fools game to romanticize the highlight reel. The measure of love isn’t in the absence of mess, it’s in your willingness to clean it up together. Again and again. Without keeping score. 

#relationships #love #connection

nombre: Baya Voce | Relationship Repair Expert | Columbia University
bio:
If you want a healthy relationship, repair is non-negotiable. I help you learn how.
Supervision w/ Esther Perel
Couples Research @columbia @maps__org
descripcion:
The moment you stop trying to fix your partner is the moment your relationship actually starts working.

Here’s what actually shifts everything: learning to see your partner’s resistance as information, not defiance.

The couples who last aren’t the ones who never trigger each other. They’re the ones who got curious about what lives underneath the trigger instead of trying to eliminate it. They learned that your partner’s “”flaws”” are usually their survival strategies.

Your nervous system wants safety, but your soul wants growth. The magic happens when you can hold both without making your partner responsible for either.

#relationships #love #growth

nombre: Baya Voce | Relationship Repair Expert | Columbia University
bio:
If you want a healthy relationship, repair is non-negotiable. I help you learn how.
Supervision w/ Esther Perel
Couples Research @columbia @maps__org
descripcion:
Chasing them feels like love, but for them it just feels like pressure. And pressure kills desire faster than the breakup ever did.

#breakup #healing #growth

#RelationshipAdvice #CommunicationSkills #couplestherapy

nombre: Baya Voce | Relationship Repair Expert | Columbia University
bio:

#relationshipadvice

nombre: Baya Voce | Relationship Repair Expert | Columbia University
bio:
If you want a healthy relationship, repair is non-negotiable. I help you learn how.
Supervision w/ Esther Perel
Couples Research @columbia @maps__org
descripcion:
What breaks a relationship is bringing something painful to someone and watching them turn it into a debate about whether you’re right to feel it.

You can love a person completely and still not be able to build anything with them if they can’t let your pain land.

#relationships #attachmentstyle #emotionalintelligence

descripcion:

#RelationshipAdvice #CouplesTherapy #AttachmentStyles

nombre: Baya Voce | Relationship Repair Expert | Columbia University
bio:
If you want a healthy relationship, repair is non-negotiable. I help you learn how.
Supervision w/ Esther Perel
Couples Research @columbia @maps__org
descripcion:
There’s a certain point where you stop being in a rough patch and start just being in the relationship. Most people spend years figuring out which one it is.

#RelationshipAdvice #SelfAwareness #PersonalGrowth

nombre: Baya Voce | Relationship Repair Expert | Columbia University
bio:
If you want a healthy relationship, repair is non-negotiable. I help you learn how.
Supervision w/ Esther Perel
Couples Research @columbia @maps__org
descripcion:
There are people who love each other deeply and still can’t make it work. The question is whether you’re actually reaching each other  and whether what you have is worth mourning what you won’t.

Most couples just keep trying to communicate their way out of a decision.

#relationships #couplestherapy #selfawareness

nombre: Baya Voce | Relationship Repair Expert | Columbia University
bio:
If you want a healthy relationship, repair is non-negotiable. I help you learn how.
Supervision w/ Esther Perel
Couples Research @columbia @maps__org
descripcion:
Nobody tells you that emotional regulation looks like sitting in a room wanting to crawl out of your skin and saying nothing anyway. It’s the decision to stay when everything in you wants to run.

#regulation #relationships

nombre: Baya Voce | Relationship Repair Expert | Columbia University
bio:
If you want a healthy relationship, repair is non-negotiable. I help you learn how.
Supervision w/ Esther Perel
Couples Research @columbia @maps__org
descripcion:
If you can’t leave the narcissist in your life, stop trying to get them to see your perspective. Well, they won’t, and chasing that will destroy you.

There’s a strategy for this, and it starts with becoming the most boring person in the room.

Have you ever had to use this with someone in your life? 👇

#narcissistic #relationships

nombre: Baya Voce | Relationship Repair Expert | Columbia University
bio:

The moment someone says “you hurt me” and the other person hears “you’re a bad person”, that’s where every repair attempt dies before it even starts.

Has this ever happened in one of your relationships? 👇

#RelationshipAdvice #CouplesTherapy #AttachmentStyles

bio:
Helping you unlock your potential through masterful communication 🚀
Free 2 Hour Training 👇
descripcion:
The way you speak changes how people perceive you!

People determine your personality through the way you play with your voice.

If you speak in a serious tone all the time, they'll take you to be a serious individual. 

If you always speak with a slightly angry tone, they'll perceive you to be aggressive.

Whereas if you learn how to speak with lots of vocal variety and melody, they'll perceive you to be dynamic, fun person.

What is the way you speak communicating about you?

bio:
Helping you unlock your potential through masterful communication 🚀
Free 2 Hour Training 👇
descripcion:
Most people think pausing makes them look uncertain.

The opposite is true.

When you pause, you give your words weight. You let the listener catch up. You show you're in control of the room, not racing to fill silence because you're afraid of it.

Here's the thing though: if you pause and your body language screams discomfort, the pause will feel awkward. But if you pause and stay grounded, shoulders back, eyes steady, the pause becomes powerful.

Try this: next time you're speaking, finish a thought, then pause for two full seconds before moving on. Don't fill it. el like what you're saying actually matters.

Comment PAUSE if you want to watch my free training on communication frameworks :)

bio:

descripcion:
Start with connection always.

When you've made it to the job interview, most of the time that means you've got the skills.

Now they want to see if they like you as a person. And what better way to show this than through answering the first question of every interview with an origin story that deepens connection with the interviewer?

bio:
Helping you unlock your potential through masterful communication 🚀
Free 2 Hour Training 👇
descripcion:
I was SO CLOSE! 😬

Comment 'CHAT' if you want my free 2 hour training to stop rambling and speak more clearly.

These are the tips I shared: 
 
1. The voice you have right now isn't your natural voice it's your habitual voice.

2. People form beliefs about you not just based on how you look, but also how you sound. You can buy the way you look, but you can't buy the way you sound. That's earnt.

3. Stop talking at the same speed. Slow down when saying something important, otherwise speed up.

4. Don't rush to answer a question. Pause first - it makes you sound more thoughtful.

ecord a video of yourself speaking for 5 minutes and review it- self awareness is the first step to improving the way you speak

6. End every sentences on a low pitch, it makes you sound more certain.

7. Your posture introduces you before your words do.

8. You become what you practice.

9. Match the expression on your face with the emotion behind your words.

10. Stop trying to be interesting. Learn how to be more interested.

11. Don't report your stories, relive them.

12. Stop replying to people with Yes But - start saying YES AND

13. What you don't change about your communication skills, you are choosing...

14. Your voice is an instrument - you don't use an instrument, you PLAY an instrument - so learn how to play it.

15. Learn how to be vulnerable without bringing the mood down.

Which one's your favorite tip?!

bio:
Helping you unlock your potential through masterful communication 🚀
Free 2 Hour Training 👇
descripcion:
Never answer a question quickly.

When someone asks you something, the worst thing you can do is rush straight into your answer. Because when you rush, you look nervous. When you look nervous, you lose credibility. And when you lose credibility, your answer doesn't land the way it should.

Here's what to do instead: pause. Take a breath. Let the question settle for just a second or two. That pause does three things. First, it gives you time to organise your thoughts. Second, it signals confidence because confident people don't need to fill every silence. Third, it makes the other person feel heard because you're actually considering what they asked.

Most people think pausing makes them look slow or unsure. It's the opposite. The pause makes you look thoughtful, controlled, and present.

Next time someone asks you a question, try this. Pause. Breathe. Then answer. You'll notice the difference immediately, and so will they.

descripcion:
Social media strategy. Funnels. Claude prompts.

Most business growth advice you hear online will have you obsessing over the same part of your business:

The part you can do.

And yes, those things matter.

But they are not what builds an 8-figure business.

Your team does.

The people who surround you. Support you. Make decisions. Protect the standard. Bring your vision to life when you are not in the room.

And your ability to lead that team is often what separates a business that depends on you from a business that can scale beyond you.

Because to reach 8 figures, growth is no longer about how much more you can personally do.

It is about how much ownership, judgment, and execution your leadership can create in others.

In today’s Substack, I break down the one thing that may be holding you back from building a business that can scale without making you the center of every decision.

Comment SUBSTACK to read the article.

#leadershipcommunication

I never met a successful CEO who was a bad communicator. Ever. Not once. 

And I’ve been in rooms with a lot of them. 

The ones scaling past $5M, $8M, $10M without burning out, they’re not smarter. 

They don’t have better teams. 

They communicate in a way that makes their presence optional. 

Their team decides without them.

Their standards hold without them. 

Their business runs without them in the room. 

That’s not luck. It’s a communication system. And it’s learnable.

If you want to learn how to level up your CEO skills your business deserves, you’ve found the right account. 

Follow if you’re building past $5M and done being the bottleneck.

descripcion:
Most leaders lose the room before they even finish announcing the change because they lead with logic when their team is still processing the emotional impact.

The data matters.
The strategy matters.
The business case matters.

But your team is first asking: What does this mean for *me*?

When you acknowledge the resistance before you justify the decision, people can actually hear you.

That is the difference between communicating like an expert and leading like a leader.

Take the Communication Ceiling Quiz to find out what is limiting your leadership communication right now.

Comment QUIZ and I’ll send it to you.

#leadershipcommunication

descripcion:
You don’t have a people problem.

You have an ownership transfer problem.

Because delegation isn’t assigning a task and hoping your team figures it out.

It’s communicating the outcome, the why, the deadline, and the decision-making lane clearly enough that they can move without you.

And if your team constantly needs reminders, explanations, follow-ups, or last-minute rescues…

That’s not just frustrating.

It’s a sign your communication may be keeping you in operator mode instead of helping you lead like the CEO.

Comment QUIZ

I’ll send you the Communication Ceiling Assessment so you can identify the communication pattern that’s keeping your team dependent on you.

#leadershipcommunication

bio:
Lawyer → VP → Founder
Your team isn't the problem. Your leadership system is.
I help 7-fig founders lead without being the bottleneck.
Start Here👇
descripcion:
Are you leading as the expert or are you leading as the CEO? 

One keeps you stuck as the bottleneck.  The other helps your team execute without needing you in every decision. 

Here’s what this looks like in real life. 

A team member comes to you because they’re struggling to execute something you already explained. 

The expert says, “Here’s how I would do it.” And then you walk them through it, fix half of it yourself, and accidentally become the answer again. 

The CEO says, “What outcome are you responsible for here?” 

The expert jumps in when something feels off. The CEO builds the standard before confusion starts. 

And this is where so many business owners get trapped. The very instincts that made you successful, jumping in, fixing quickly, seeing what others miss, those are the same instincts creating dependency as the business grows. 

But at a certain stage, your business no longer needs you to be the smartest person in every conversation. It needs you to lead. 

If you want to know where your communication is keeping you in expert mode, comment QUIZ and I’ll send you the free Communication Ceiling Assessment.

#leadershipcommunication

descripcion:
“I can’t find good people. People don’t want to work anymore.”

I had met one of those VPs. She was a rockstar.

The problem wasn’t her inability to work. It was the cycle the CEO was stuck in:

He kept everything in his head. She guessed. She guessed wrong. He corrected. Trust broke. She disengaged. He micromanaged. She left.

Three VPs. Same cycle.

The fix was never better candidates. It was learning to say three things:

“Here’s what success looks like for this role.”
“Here’s how I think, so you’re never left guessing.”
“Here’s what I need from you daily and why it matters.”

Finding A-players is rarely the problem. Keeping them is.

#leadershipcommunication

Every time you swoop in and fix, you think you’re being a good leader.

You’re not.

You’re teaching your team that their job is to find the problem and bring it to you.

And your job is to solve it.

That is not leadership. 

That is a Communication Ceiling and it is the reason your A-players keep delivering B-results.

The good news: it is not a people problem!

It is a communication pattern. And patterns can be broken.

Comment QUIZ and I’ll send you the free assessment that identifies exactly which Communication Ceiling is keeping you stuck as the fixer in your own company.

descripcion:
Managing up is not about flattery. It’s about strategy. The most influential leaders know how to deliver feedback upwards without losing their credibility. 

Here’s how.

That conversation you’re dreading with your boss? It’s the one that could define your leadership trajectory.

Most professionals either stay silent and let problems fester, or they deliver feedback as criticism, putting their careers at risk. 

But there is a third, more powerful option.

To truly transition from an expert to a leader, you must learn to frame feedback as a strategic blind spot, not a personal failure. 

It’s about positioning yourself as an indispensable ally, not a subordinate judge.

When you provide the information they need to succeed, you’re no longer a threat: you’re a high-value partner. 

You stop being managed and start influencing.

This is how you build influence and make a real impact.

#leadershipcommunication

descripcion:
Jessica had been in that company for six years.

She built the onboarding program from scratch. She trained half the managers on her floor. Her team had the lowest attrition in the department for three years running.

She found out about the promotion on a Tuesday morning.
Not in a meeting with her manager.

In the company newsletter.

The name wasn’t hers.

It was Suzelle’s. 

Suzelle, who had been there eighteen months. 
Suzelle who came to Jessica when she struggled with her team.

When she came to me she said: I don’t understand what I’m doing wrong.

And after a complete assessment I told her: you’re amazing at your job and you’re just communicating at the level of the role you *have* instead of the role you *want*.

Every time Jessica walked into a room she showed them how well she managed her current responsibilities.

Suzelle walked in and talked about where the business was going.

Same meeting. Completely different message.

They didn’t pass her over because she wasn’t good enough.
They passed her over because she reinforced her current position.

Not a single glimpse of who she was capable of becoming.

If you’re feeling this right now, comment EXPERT and I’ll send you details on our upcoming training.

As for Jessica, she’s been invited to an executive meeting next month and we’re getting her ready for a skip-level meeting to prep her next promotion. 👏🏼

#leadershipcommunication

descripcion:
You got promoted because you were the best at doing the work.

Now you’re being paid to stop doing it.

But nobody taught you how to lead people who do what you used to do so you default to what got you here: telling people exactly what to do and how to do it.

That’s not leadership. That’s just management with a better title.

The shift from expert to executive isn’t about knowing more.

It’s about communicating differently:  less “here’s what to do,” more “here’s where we’re going.”

Direction creates leaders under you.
Tasks create order-takers.

If you’re ready to make that shift, comment EXPERT below.

The Expert to Leader Challenge is coming in April and it only runs a few times a year.

#leader #executive #leadershipcommunication
